meta.title: Agent 365 vs Azure AI Foundry — Interactive Comparison
meta.description: Side-by-side comparison of Microsoft's AI agent management and development platforms

hero.subtitle: Two platforms. Different missions. One lets you manage AI agents at enterprise scale. The other lets you build them from scratch.

sections.label: Core Identity
sections.title: Two Sides of the AI Agent Coin
sections.description: Think of Agent 365 as the command center and Foundry as the workshop. One governs. One creates.

items.title: Centralized Dashboard
items.description: Single pane of glass to monitor every AI agent across your M365 tenant

items.title: Identity & Access
items.description: Entra-integrated RBAC, agent identity management, permission boundaries

items.title: Policy Enforcement
items.description: Org-wide policies, compliance rules, approved agent registries

items.title: Audit & Observability
items.description: Full activity logs, lifecycle tracking, who created what and when

items.title: Security Integration
items.description: Defender, Purview, Intune — treat AI agents like first-class security principals

items.title: No/Low-Code
items.description: Configuration-driven management, extensible via Copilot Studio plugins

items.title: Code-First Platform
items.description: SDKs, APIs, and a developer studio for maximum flexibility

items.title: Model Control
items.description: Bring your own model, fine-tune, customize prompts, full RAG pipelines

items.title: Advanced Orchestration
items.description: Concurrent fan-out/fan-in, multi-agent workflows, parallel execution

items.title: Experimentation
items.description: A/B testing, evaluation frameworks, prompt engineering tools

items.title: Open Ecosystem
items.description: Third-party APIs, open-source models, custom connectors

items.title: Azure-Scale Infrastructure
items.description: PaaS deployment, auto-scaling, global regions, enterprise SLAs

sections.label: Head-to-Head
sections.title: Category-by-Category Breakdown
sections.description: Click a category to see how they stack up in each area.

left.title: 🛡️ Agent 365
left.description: IT administrators, security teams, compliance officers. People who manage and secure technology.

right.title: 🏗️ Azure AI Foundry
right.description: Professional developers, data scientists, ML engineers. People who build and deploy AI solutions.

left.title: 🛡️ Agent 365
left.description: SaaS — Microsoft-managed. No infrastructure to provision. Agents governed within your M365 tenant. Flip-a-switch simplicity.

right.title: 🏗️ Azure AI Foundry
right.description: PaaS — Azure-managed, you control. You provision resources, pick regions, configure networking. Full control, more responsibility.

left.title: 🛡️ Agent 365
left.description: Centralized, org-wide. Tenant-level policy enforcement, Entra identity, Purview compliance, Defender protection. Governance is the primary purpose.

right.title: 🏗️ Azure AI Foundry
right.description: Workload-level, custom. Azure RBAC, app-level permissions, custom guardrails in code. Governance is your responsibility.

left.title: 🛡️ Agent 365
left.description: Sequential, low-code. Copilot Studio-style orchestration with limits on session duration and concurrency.

right.title: 🏗️ Azure AI Foundry
right.description: True concurrent orchestration. Fan-out/fan-in, parallel multi-agent execution, complex DAGs, real-time processing.

left.title: 🛡️ Agent 365
left.description: Plugins & Studio. Extend via Copilot Studio declarative agents, Power Platform connectors, Work IQ integrations.

right.title: 🏗️ Azure AI Foundry
right.description: Full custom code. Any SDK, any API, any model. Python, .NET, REST APIs, open-source frameworks — limitless.

left.title: 🛡️ Agent 365
left.description: Abstracted, Microsoft-managed. You don't pick or tune models — Microsoft handles it. Simpler but less control.

right.title: 🏗️ Azure AI Foundry
right.description: Full control. Azure OpenAI, Llama, Mistral, Phi, custom models. Fine-tune, adjust parameters, build custom RAG.

left.title: 🛡️ Agent 365
left.description: Entra-aware, Work IQ. Permission-aware grounding via Microsoft Graph, SharePoint, OneDrive. Respects M365 access controls.

right.title: 🏗️ Azure AI Foundry
right.description: Azure AI Search + Foundry IQ. Custom vector stores, hybrid search, permission-aware RAG, custom embeddings.

sections.label: Architecture
sections.title: How They Fit Together
sections.description: Build in Foundry. Manage with Agent 365. They're complementary layers of the same stack.

sections.label: Decision Guide
sections.title: Which One Do You Need?
sections.description: Answer these questions to find your path. Many organizations use both.

cards.question: "I need to monitor all AI agents across my org"
"I need to build a custom multi-agent system"
"I need compliance and audit trails for agents"
"I need to fine-tune models and use custom data"
"I want to secure agents like I secure users"

options.label: ✅ Use Both Together
options.sublabel: Build in Foundry → Manage in Agent 365

sections.label: Pricing
sections.title: How They're Licensed
sections.description: Different pricing models for different use cases.

sections.label: Better Together
sections.title: Not Either/Or — Both
sections.description: The most mature organizations will use both platforms as complementary layers.

items.title: Build in Foundry
items.description: Developers create custom AI agents with full model control, RAG pipelines, and advanced orchestration

items.title: Register in Agent 365
items.description: Agents are registered in the enterprise agent registry for visibility and lifecycle management

items.title: Govern & Secure
items.description: IT applies policies, sets boundaries, integrates with Entra, Defender, and Purview for compliance

items.title: Monitor at Scale
items.description: Agent 365 provides ongoing observability — audit trails, anomaly detection, and usage analytics
